opportunities.title: Jimothy the Raccoon Has Official D&D Stats Now — Here's the Ruling on Trash-Panda Grapple Checks
opportunities.why_now: EN World just published official stats for a real viral raccoon, giving a built-in creature-stat-block gag with real crunch to dissect.
opportunities.angle: Creature tactic short: read the stat block, mock/praise it, then give your own house ruling for how a CR-whatever raccoon should actually fight (advantage on Sleight of Hand vs. unattended food, Nimble Escape analog).

opportunities.title: 'Cloaks and Daggers' Ruling: Does Hiding Behind a Cloak Actually Grant Stealth in 5e/Shadowdark?
opportunities.why_now: EN World's new piece on cloak-and-dagger table tactics is a direct prompt for a rules-ruling short on concealment vs. cover mechanics.
opportunities.angle: One-ruling short: break down RAW on cloaks/darkness for Hide checks in 5e vs. Shadowdark's binary light rules, give a table-ready ruling.

opportunities.title: Free Adventure Autopsy: Is 'Thief of Princes' Actually Runnable in One Session?
opportunities.why_now: A brand-new free adventure just dropped this week — perfect timely content to mine for a quick GM breakdown before anyone else covers it.
opportunities.angle: Fast module-review short: pull one clever encounter or trap from the PDF, show how to port it into Shadowdark/DCC with minimal conversion math.

opportunities.title: Chaosium's Innsmouth Book Just Dropped — Here's the DCC-Legal Way to Steal Its Best Monster
opportunities.why_now: Chaosium's new definitive Innsmouth guide is fresh news and a goldmine for Lovecraftian content DCC GMs already lean into.
opportunities.angle: Spell/creature use-case short: pick one Deep One or ritual from the book and show the quick stat conversion for DCC or a 5e homebrew stat block.

opportunities.title: 'A Spoonful of Sugar Helps the Mayhem Go Down' — The Actual Ruling on Running Chaotic Combat Without Losing the Table
opportunities.why_now: EN World's column on managing mayhem at the table is a direct prompt for a mechanical fix rather than just GM philosophy.
opportunities.angle: Dry mechanical short: turn the soft advice into a hard ruling — e.g., a house rule for initiative chaos or morale checks that actually resolves 'mayhem' instead of just describing it.
